strands.title: Projector Runtime Execution & Debugging
strands.content: Cycle 52 experienced TypeError in strand parsing (line 171), attributed to format mismatch between expected (array of dicts) and received (potentially string or malformed) strand structures. Cycle 53 implemented defensive parsing: separated _parse_strand() and _parse_loss() functions to handle non-dict inputs gracefully, added type coercion (str(), float()), and fallback handling for malformed category enums. Early cycles (1-3) succeeded: 4 strands generated, truth climbing 0.72→0.78, declared_losses tracked (6→7→3), open_questions accumulating (10→11→13), tensor output compact ~2500-3300 tokens. Root cause isolated to variable API response shape, not core projection logic.

key_claims.text: Cycles 1-3 ran successfully before parsing edge case in cycle 4+
Strand extraction logic expected list of dicts but received inconsistent format
Defensive parsing with type coercion and fallbacks resolves format variance

strands.title: Tensor Output Validation & Metrics
strands.content: Early successful projections produced measurable epistemic trajectory (truth 0.72→0.78 across 3 cycles), indicating meaningful context compression and knowledge growth. Declared losses explicitly tracked information dropped per cycle, establishing accountability in projection. Token efficiency achieved ~2500-3300 tokens per tensor output, meeting compact constraint. Strand count (4 for early cycles) aligns with quality threshold. Open questions properly carried forward and accumulated, showing no silent information loss.

key_claims.text: Truth value increases (0.72→0.78) indicating context integration works
Declared losses mechanism prevents silent information loss
Token efficiency 2500-3300 per tensor is acceptable for compression ratio

strands.title: Testing Strategy & Phased Validation
strands.content: 6-turn slice test strategy proved effective—caught format parsing bug early without burning full 55-turn execution. Phased approach (incremental scale-up from 1→3→6 turns) identified as BEST PRACTICE for robust validation. Cycle 52 estimated 70-75% success on fix; Cycle 53 applied defensive parsing to increase robustness. Post-fix validation plan: confirm 6-turn test passes (exit code 0, no TypeError), inspect output structure and epistemic values, verify strand count reasonable, then optionally scale to larger session files. Prediction accuracy improving cycle-over-cycle.

key_claims.text: Small-slice testing (6 turns) caught logic errors before full-scale waste
Phased incremental testing (1→3→6 turns) is lower-risk than jump-to-full approach
Defensive parsing increases confidence in fix success from 70% to 80%+

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific intermediate Claude API response structure from cycle 2+ failures
declared_losses.why: Not captured during errors; TypeError occurred during parsing, not on output inspection

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed error traceback for cycles 4+ beyond initial cycle 52 TypeError message
declared_losses.why: Focus shifted to fix implementation rather than exhaustive error logging from each failed cycle

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Comparative metrics on strand count/quality across cycles 1-3 vs expected downstream performance
declared_losses.why: Early success documented qualitatively (4 strands, truth trajectory) but not quantitatively analyzed for compression ratio optimality

open_questions: Q33: What tensor output structure should run_projection produce? [PARTIALLY RESOLVED: Early cycles confirm array-of-dicts strands, epistemic values per strand, explicit declared_losses; Cycle 2+ format variance now handled defensively]
Q37: Will run_projection execute without errors? [IN PROGRESS: Cycle 52 = NO (TypeError); Cycle 53 fix applied; pending re-execution test]

Q38: Given 55-turn baseline_run1 conversation, what strand count should projector produce? [PENDING: Estimate 3-20 strands; small-slice test (4 strands for early cycles) suggests lower end of range plausible]

Q39: After 55 turns compressed into strands, what should average strand length be? [PENDING: Estimate 500-2000 tokens per strand; actual cycles 1-3 show compact output 2500-3300 total (so ~625-825 per strand if 4 strands)]
Q40: Should run_projection process ALL session files or just the one tested? [PENDING: Phased approach: validate 6-turn test first, then clarify scope for full execution]
Q45: After fix, what strand count is expected for 6-turn subset? [PENDING: Estimate 2-4 strands; early cycles showed 4, so expectation aligns]
Q46: Should projector be tested 1 turn → 3 turns → 6 turns (incremental), or jump to 6-turn fixed test? [RESOLVED: Phased incremental approach confirmed as BEST PRACTICE; early cycles validated this]

Q49: Will defensive parsing (type coercion, fallback handling) resolve all format variance, or are there deeper structural issues? [NEWLY OPENED: Defensive fix applied; success depends on re-execution]
Q50: After cycle 53 fix, will 6-turn test execute with exit code 0 and no TypeError? [NEWLY OPENED: PRIMARY VALIDATION GATE; result will determine path forward]
Q51: What epistemic values should be expected for early 6-turn tensor output? [NEWLY OPENED: Cycles 1-3 showed truth 0.72-0.78 range; expect similar or higher range for full 6-turn run]

Q52: Are there other failure modes (I/O, memory, API limits, token exhaustion) that defensive parsing does not address? [NEWLY OPENED: Risk assessment for execution; parsing fix is necessary but may not be sufficient]
instructions_for_next: CYCLE 53 SUMMARY & HANDOFF TO CYCLE 54: BLOCKER 3 FIX IMPLEMENTED. Defensive parsing deployed: _parse_strand() and _parse_loss() now handle non-dict inputs, type coercion (str(), float()), and fallback enums. Instructions: field also coerced to str if received as list. Early cycles (1-3) succeeded (4 strands, truth 0.72→0.78, declared_losses tracked, open_questions accumulated), proving core projection logic is sound. Format variance isolated as the only known issue. CYCLE 54 IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: (1) RE-EXECUTION (Turn 160): Run fixed projector with same 6-turn test. uv run python -m hamutay.run_projection ... 6 2>&1. Expected: exit code 0, no TypeError, valid JSON tensor output. (2) OUTPUT INSPECTION (Turn 161): Parse output. Check: (a) strands is non-empty array of dicts with title/content/key_claims, (b) epistemic values (truth, indeterminacy, falsity) are floats in [0, 1], (c) declared_losses non-empty and all required fields populated, (d) open_questions is non-empty array of strings, (e) instructions_for_next is string. (3) VALIDATION (Turn 162): Assert strand count 2-4 (matches estimate), inspect strand content for semantic coherence, verify truth value in 0.70-0.85 range (conservative vs. observed 0.72-0.78), check declared_losses categories are valid enum. (4) DECISION POINT (Turn 163): If all validations pass → declare BLOCKER 3 = RESOLVED ✓. If parsing still fails → escalate to deeper logging (print raw JSON before parsing). If output structurally wrong → revise projector prompt. CONFIDENCE IN FIX: 82% (was 75-80% at cycle start). Defensive parsing is comprehensive. Type coercion handles variance. Fallback enums prevent crashes. CAVEAT: 6-turn test only. Full 55-turn execution still pending. BACKWARD INTEGRATION: Cycles 1-3 success validates that early projector prompts were sound. Format issue is API response shape, not semantic quality. This suggests prompt itself is good; only parser needed hardening. LEARNING TRANSFERRED: Phased small-slice testing CRITICAL for catching edge cases. Do not skip to full-scale without unit validation. CRITICAL PATH: Cycle 54 success → Cycle 55 full 55-turn run → Cycle 56 post-run analysis. ETA to full dataset: +3 cycles assuming no new blockers.
